attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put two phones on the dresser at the foot of the bed.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a bedroom environment with a bed, a dresser, and various objects placed on them. The floor is made of light brown parquet wood.

1. Objects and their types:
   - Two smartphones are present in the scene. These are classified as "object" (not receptacle) per the domain definition.
   - A wooden dresser with drawers is located at the foot of the bed. It is a "receptacle" because it can hold objects on its surface.
   - A bed with an orange patterned bedspread is visible. The bed itself is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, but its surface can temporarily hold objects.
   - A lamp, a clock, a pen, and a set of keys are on the dresser. These are all "object" types.
   - A bowl is on a separate wooden surface (possibly a shelf or table) in one view, but not relevant to the current task.

2. Quantities:
   - Smartphones: 2
   - Dresser: 1
   - Lamp: 1
   - Clock: 1
   - Pen: 1
   - Keys: 1 set

3. Spatial relationships:
   - One smartphone is currently on the bedspread, near the left side of the bed.
   - The second smartphone is on a wooden surface (possibly a shelf or table) in a separate view, not on the dresser.
   - The dresser is positioned at the foot of the bed, as specified in the instruction.
   - The lamp, clock, pen, and keys are all on top of the dresser.
   - The bowl is on a separate wooden surface and is not on the dresser or bed.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - Neither smartphone is currently on the dresser.
   - The dresser is not openable (not a container with a lid or door), so it does not have an "opened" state.
   - No objects are currently held by the agent (robot).
   - The smartphones are not clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled — these predicates are irrelevant for phones in this context.
   - The agent is not at any object’s location initially.

5. Colors and positions:
   - The bedspread is orange with a dark brown geometric pattern.
   - The smartphones are black with silver or white bezels.
   - The dresser is made of light brown wood with visible drawer handles.
   - The lamp on the dresser has a yellow lampshade and a dark base.
   - The clock is round with a gray body and blue face.
   - The pen is white with a black tip.
   - The keys are metallic with a black keychain.

The task requires the robot to move both smartphones from their current locations (one on the bed, one on a separate surface) and place them on the dresser at the foot of the bed. The dresser is the target receptacle for this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_phones_on_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        phone1 phone2 - object
        dresser - receptacle
        bed - object
        lamp clock pen keys - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed)
        (inReceptacle phone1 bed)
        (atLocation robot lamp)
        (inReceptacle phone2 lamp)
        (inReceptacle lamp dresser)
        (inReceptacle clock dresser)
        (inReceptacle pen dresser)
        (inReceptacle keys dresser)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle phone1 dresser)
        (inReceptacle phone2 dresser)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_phones_on_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        phone1 phone2 - object
        dresser - receptacle
        bed - object
        lamp clock pen keys - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot bed)
        (inReceptacle phone1 bed)
        (atLocation robot lamp)
        (inReceptacle phone2 lamp)
        (inReceptacle lamp dresser)
        (inReceptacle clock dresser)
        (inReceptacle pen dresser)
        (inReceptacle keys dresser)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle phone1 dresser)
        (inReceptacle phone2 dresser)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot phone1)
(pickupobject robot phone1)
(gotolocation robot dresser)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot phone1 dresser)
(gotolocation robot phone2)
(pickupobject robot phone2)
(gotolocation robot dresser)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot phone2 dresser)
